text.title: Django + Celery Background Jobs Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the django-celery methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale; the final rule is the skip-this-methodology gate referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: Tasks dispatched from a transaction MUST use transaction.on_commit (or task.apply_async via on_commit hook). Bare .delay() inside @atomic is forbidden.
rule.rationale: .delay() can execute before the commit completes, causing 'task fired but row does not exist' incidents.

rule.statement: Every task accepts a business id (order_id, refund_id) and is idempotent under that key. Retrying with the same key is a no-op or returns the same result.
rule.rationale: Celery guarantees at-least-once delivery; non-idempotent tasks cause duplicate charges/emails on retry.

rule.statement: Tasks declare max_retries (≤5) and use exponential backoff with full jitter. Tasks without a retry policy use a project-wide default.

rationale.source: AWS Architecture Blog — exponential backoff + jitter.

rule.rationale: Unbounded retries pin worker slots forever on a sick downstream; full jitter avoids retry storms.

rule.statement: After max_retries the task acks to a dead-letter queue with the original payload + last exception. DLQ has an on-call alert.
rule.rationale: Silent drops hide failures for days; DLQ + alert ensures human attention.

rule.statement: Task arguments are JSON-serialisable primitives or model ids — never ORM instances or Decimals without conversion.
rule.rationale: Pickling ORM objects breaks across migrations and across worker versions.

rule.statement: Each Django app owns its tasks.py; cross-app effects go via the contracts module, not by importing another app's task.
rule.rationale: Cross-app task imports replicate the tight coupling decomposition was meant to fix.

rule.statement: If task volume < 100/day OR no broker is set up OR job dispatch must be sub-second, skip this methodology.
rule.rationale: Celery overhead is wasted at low volume and unsuitable for tight-latency dispatch.
